Show Hide Cancel Next Enable Disable Close OK Done Retry Change Add Confirm Refresh Connect Reject Disconnect Create Advanced Hardware wallet Hardware wallet (no NFC detected) No NFC reader detected, enable to use hardware cards Offline activation Card authenticity check cannot reach the server. Check your internet connection or allow offline activation. Offline activation skips server verification and reduces security assurance. Continue with offline activation? Continue offline Import Reset I Understand Switch Remove Save I Agree Continue Send Search Back Copy Browser Delete Learn More Activate Later Sign Try Again Share Apply Set Amount Continue Anyway Info Not enough TON for fee. Your current balance is %s TON. Make sure you have at least %s for fees or the transaction may fail The recipient hasn\'t enabled %s, therefore they are unable to receive it.
Can\'t be less than %1$s or more than %2$s Wrong integer number Error n/a - Any Sync error. Try Again Unknown send error Unable to Start A required component failed to load. Please try reinstalling the app from the Play Store. Close
© PirateCash and Cosanta Foundation 2018-%1$d
Keyboard
Note This wallet seems to use a non-standard character in its recovery phrase and/or a passphrase. If you do not see a balance or transactions please read below for details.\n\nCLICK TO GET MORE INFO This is a non-standard wallet. \n\nCLICK TO GET MORE INFO
DEX Messenger Chat freely and without restrictions (will be available in future updates). Go Borderless Bypass conditional barriers and access markets globally. Stay Private Do not leak your private and financial data to the world.
I Understand
Advanced Recovery Phrase Word List Passphrase Password Confirm %s words (recommended) %s words Restore Height Restore Height or Date (YYYY-MM-DD) %s words (Monero only) Passphrase cannot be empty Please use only supported symbols: A-Z a-z 0-9 \' " ` & / ? ! : ; . , ~ * $ = + - [ ](){} <> \ _ # @ | % Passphrase confirmation does not match Failed to create wallet. Please try again or restart the app. Passphrases add additional security layer for wallets. To restore such wallet a user required both a recovery phrase as well as a passphrase.\n\nPassphrases also make it easy for users to have many multi-coin wallets using a single mnemonic but different passwords.
English Japanese Korean Spanish Chinese (Simplified) Chinese (Traditional) French Italian Czech Portuguese
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Enjoying P.CASH? Share feedback and ideas to take it further. Rate It Not now
Advanced Wrong words count. It must be between 12 or 24 words. You entered: %d Wrong words count. It must be 25 words. You entered: %d Invalid height Invalid height, make sure you entered YYYY-MM-DD or birth height Invalid QR Code Can\'t connect to Blockchair to find the height by date Monero restore error Passphrase cannot be empty Invalid Checksum Enter the passphrase you used when creating this recovery phrase. Restoring an existing wallet with a new passphrase will generate a brand new wallet. Choose Blockchains Import By Import By Recovery Phrase Private Key Enter EVM Private Key, BIP32 Root Key, Stellar Secret Key or Account Extended Private Key Enter Recovery Phrase Monero restore seed Non-Standard Import This page provides a special wallet restore mechanism to P.CASH users who have a non-standard wallet. Such wallets might have been created in P.CASH version 0.27 - 0.28 using a non-English recovery phrase and/or a special character in the wallet passphrase (i.e. diacritic symbol).\n\nIf you\'re an affected user then your wallet balance will be shown 0 after restoring such wallet in version 0.29 or above. This page will enable you to restore access to your non-standard wallet. Once restored you\'re recommended to create a new wallet (which will be standard compliant) and move funds there.
(recommended)
Invalid Key
All
Birthday Height or Date Birthday Height Monero ZCash Enter wallet\'s birthday height for faster synchronization. 000000000 (optional) Restore as New New Wallet Doesn\'t have any transactions Existing Wallet Has transactions The initial synchronization with the blockchain can consume a lot of internet traffic.
MCap Vol Price Markets Search RECENT POPULAR MARKET PARAMETERS PRICE PARAMETERS NETWORK PARAMETERS INDICATORS Highest Cap Lowest Cap Highest Volume Lowest Volume Sort by BTC Dominance 24h Volume See All Powered By CoinGeko API News Coins Platforms Pairs Sectors Watchlist Your watchlist is empty. Top Coins Signals Volume Market Cap TVL Below signals are based on the Bollinger Bands and RSI technical price indicators over approx. the last 30 days. These signals are algorithmic and can change frequently. High confidence in price increase. Likely price increase in near future. No clear trend, market is in equilibrium. Likely price decrease in near future. High probability of price decrease. Elevated risk level, requires caution. Always remember to apply risk management, and note that this is not financial advice. Top Market Pairs Sectors
Top Platforms Protocols: %s %s Ecosystem Market cap of all protocols on the %s chain

Total Net Inflow The net inflow of an ETF equals its cash inflows minus outflows. Highest Assets Lowest Assets Inflow Outflow Total Net Assets

Multi-Chain
Balance Send Shield and consolidate funds Receive Address %1$s per %2$s until %s Syncing… Connecting… Searching transactions… %s tx Syncing… %s%% %s blocks remaining Processing… Waiting for peers: %1$d of %2$d Connecting to API… Balance Sort by Name Price Change You haven\'t added any coins to this wallet. You have no assets to send. Network Format Choose network to get an address to receive. Choose a format for providing an address to receive. Address Format The Cash Address format is preferred for receiving Bitcoin Cash (BCH) due to its improved user experience and compatibility. However, both address formats can be used interchangeably to receive BCH, regardless of the sender\'s address format. The Native SegWit format is preferred in Bitcoin for improved throughput and security. All address formats (Taproot, SegWit, Legacy) can be used interchangeably to receive BTC regardless of the sender\'s address format, enabling seamless transactions across different coin types. Account Not active Amount Set Amount Locked Processing Broadcasting Memo (Tag) Can\'t recognize This address is the address of the watched account Used Addresses %s addresses change for privacy and security. Below a list of used addresses in this wallet. Receive Addresses Change Addresses New Used Unused Create new address Create another address? You already have a new address that hasn\'t received any funds yet. Creating a new address is not required. Do you want to continue? Don\'t ask again (save setting) Create new Keep current For enhanced privacy, it is recommended to use a new address for each transaction.\nIn our app, Monero addresses are created manually to reduce the load when scanning the wallet.\nBelow are the addresses previously created in this wallet. Coin Name Search Trustline Reserved Reserved
Send Not Enough Balance
Enter Address Send %s
Address or Domain
Fee Blockchains require users to pay network fees when sending transactions. The fees are higher when a lot of transactions are taking place on the network. Paste Max Advanced TimeLock works only for sending to BIP44 addresses (starting with 1) UTxOs UTxO Expert Mode Manually select UTxO to spend the funds in the balance Replace by Fee This option allows you to resend a stuck transaction by increasing the amount of the fee. Enabled Disabled Available Balance Unsellect All Sellect All
Confirm You Send You Receive Mint Burn To Own Method Nonce Memo Send Action
Security Report No checks available Checking address... Recent Invalid Address Detected Clear Phishing Check Smart Contract Check Analyzes the provided destination address for potential phishing risks. Clear: No suspicious activity detected. Detected: It appears that someone may be trying to trick you into sending funds to an incorrect address. Do not proceed unless absolutely certain that the address is correct. Consider sending a small test transaction first. Blacklist Check Check from alpha-aml.com Checks if the destination address is not a smart contract. Clear: Not a smart contract. Detected: The address is a smart contract. Make sure you really intend to send funds to this address. In some cases, such transfers may be irreversible or result in loss of funds. Proceed only if you\'re sure. Checks if the destination address is listed on official or unofficial blacklists. Clear: No blacklist flags. Detected: The address is blacklisted and may be associated with legal or security risks. Proceed with caution. Many crypto exchanges restrict users who deposit or withdraw funds from wallets that have interacted with blacklisted addresses. Keep that in mind. Checks if the destination address is listed in alpha-aml.com. Clear: No blacklist flags. Detected: The address is listed on alpha-aml.com and may be associated with legal or security risks. Proceed with caution. Many crypto exchanges restrict users who deposit or withdraw funds from wallets that have interacted with blacklisted addresses. Keep that in mind. Sanctions Check Verifies whether the recipient’s address is subject to international sanctions, using data from Chainalysis.com. Clear: No sanctions detected. Detected: Potential legal risk. Transactions with this address may be restricted or illegal in certain jurisdictions. The recipient address is a smart contract! Phishing Address Detected! Blacklisted Address Detected! AML Problem Detected! Sanctioned Address Detected! Make sure you really intend to send funds to this address. In some cases, such transfers may be irreversible or result in loss of funds. Proceed only if you\'re sure. The address you entered may be fraudulent. Scammers often alter the first and last characters. Verify the full address carefully before proceeding to avoid loss. The address you entered is flagged as blacklisted. Transactions to this address could lead to loss or other risks. Please verify the recipient\'s details before proceeding. The address you entered is found in the AML blacklist. Transactions to this address could lead to loss or other risks. Please verify the recipient\'s details before proceeding. The address you entered is marked as being under sanctions. Transactions to this address could lead to loss or other risks. Please verify the recipient\'s details before proceeding.
Risky Address! You are about to send funds to a high-risk address. Proceeding may result in loss or other security risks. By continuing, you accept the potential dangers. Are you sure you want to proceed?
\u2713 Sent Transaction queued, will be sent when peers connect
Camera permission Please grant the camera permission No camera permission Access to your camera for this app was denied, you can go to settings and allow access Go to Settings Camera is not available. Please try again or use a different device. %s address SMART SCAN Choose from Photos Could not read a QR code from the selected image
Time locking works only when sending to payment addresses starting with 1... (aka BIP44 addresses) Min. amount %s Max. amount %s Sending %s to your own address is not allowed. Please enter a different recipient address.
Available Balance Transaction Speed Memo Next TimeLock Off 1 hour 1 month 6 months 1 year Sending
Warning! Risk of getting stuck Fee Error Fee Rate is too low. The transaction may get stuck or fail Fee Error The connection to the server has been lost. To proceed, set up the fees manually using the data on the site.
Advanced Transaction Nonce The nonce is a unique integer value for a transaction within the user\'s wallet. It normally increments with each submitted transaction and does not need changing. Advanced users can set it equal to a nonce of a pending transaction in order to cancel and replace that transaction, as long as the new transaction has a sufficiently higher fee to prevent the old one from being confirmed instead (for example, they may want to speed up its confirmation, or to change transaction parameters entirely). When multiple pending transactions have the same nonce, only one gets confirmed, typically the one with the highest fee. Used Nonce An executed transaction with this nonce already exists.
Network Fee Fee Estimated Fee The estimated cost of sending given transaction on the network. Gas Limit Transaction complexity is measured in units called \"gas\". It varies depending on the smart contract being executed. The Gas Limit is the estimated maximum gas needed to execute it. The actual gas used will normally be lower. Gas Price (Gwei) The fee for transacting on the network is measured in gas units. Gas Price is the amount a user is willing to spend per unit of gas. When the network is busy, gas prices are high, and low when it\'s idle. An insufficient gas price is often a reason for a transaction to remain pending for an extended period. Base Fee The network protocol determines the base price per gas for each block, called base fee rate. It varies according to the network utilization level from block to block. It can increase or decrease by no more than 12.5% in the next block, making fees more predictable. The value shown here is the current block\'s base fee rate. Max Fee Rate This is the maximum total price per gas the user is willing to pay. It must cover the network\'s base fee rate and max priority fee rate. The value shown here is suggested based on an estimate of the next block\'s base fee rate plus the max priority fee rate chosen by the user. The actual fee rate paid will normally be lower. Setting this lower than the current base fee rate will limit the fee paid, but will result in longer waiting times for the transaction to be confirmed, or even in a stuck transaction. Max Priority Fee Users pay priority fees to incentivize a transaction to be confirmed more quickly. They are sometimes called tips. The max priority fee rate is the maximum additional price per gas the user is willing to pay on top of the base fee rate. The value shown here is suggested based on predicted network conditions. The actual priority fee will normally be lower. Setting this to zero may result in a long waiting time for transaction to be confirmed, as it is placed at the end of the pending transactions queue from all users. Risky The transaction may remain pending for a while or fail entirely. Risky To ensure speedy execution of the transaction the max fee rate (subtracted max. miner tip) should be higher than current base fee rate. If not transaction may remain pending until condition is met. Fee Too High The set transaction fee is higher than necessary for processing this transaction now. Fee Rate (SAT/BYTE) Here is the recommended value for hitting the next 2 blocks Blockchains require users to pay network fees when sending transactions. The fees are higher when a lot of transactions are taking place on the network. \n\nThe P.CASH wallet estimates fee based on the current blockchain activity and recommends the optimal value in order for the transaction to be processed within reasonable amount of time. \n\nThe recommended fee rate shown as the amount of satoshi user needs to pay for a single byte of the transaction. Thus, the total fee depends on the total size of the transaction which is measured in bytes. \n\nUsers may use provided controls to increase or decrease the fee rate value. The change in fee rate changes the total fee for the transaction the user will pay. \n\nSetting fee rate below recommended value may result in a transaction being held as pending for hours, or being rejected. The lower the value, the longer it will take for the transaction to confirm. For transactions where priority is important, we recommend setting higher fee rate. We couldn\'t calculate the network fee precisely. We used an approximate value, but the transaction may fail or the fee may be higher than normal.
Transaction Speed Both Bitcoin and Ethereum blockchains require users to pay network fees when sending transactions. The fees are higher when a lot of transactions are taking place on the network and lower when the blockchain is less busy. Low Selecting a value from this range may result in a transaction being held as pending for hours, or being rejected. The lower the value, the longer it will take for the transaction to confirm. Recommended Typically, the recommended fee value should be sufficient for a transaction to be processed within minutes or an hour for Bitcoin and within minutes for Ethereum. High For transactions where priority is important, we recommend using the high fee value. It\'s expected to be sufficient for a transaction to be completed within 10 minutes for Bitcoin and within 1-2 minutes for Ethereum. Fee The estimated cost of sending given transaction on the network. (Without excluding Energy, Bandwidth and Activating Fee) Activation Fee This address is not active. Transferring TRX or TRC-10 tokens to an inactive account address will activate the account. Resources Consumed Bandwidth is the unit that measures the size of the transaction bytes stored in the blockchain database. The larger the transaction, the more bandwidth resources will be consumed.\n\nEnergy is the unit that measures the amount of computation required by the TRON virtual machine to perform specific operations on the TRON network.\n\nSince smart contract transactions require computing resources to execute, each smart contract transaction requires to pay for the energy fee.
%s Network Fee You need %1$s to use %2$s %2$s runs on %3$s.\nNetwork fee is paid in %1$s.\nYour %1$s balance: %4$s The %1$s network protocol states that at least %2$s is required to pay the network fee for this transaction. You have less than %s in your balance. at least %s less than %s but and With this balance, there is a risk that the actual fee may be higher than expected. be higher than expected In this case: %s will be debited from your balance, the transaction may not be completed, the coins will not be sent to the recipient. By continuing, you confirm that: understand the specifics of how the %s network works, are aware of the risk of losing commission, accept the possible consequences of an unsuccessful transaction. Points to agree with: I understand and accept the risk
This address is not active Not Active Address Newly created accounts on the TRON blockchain are inactive and cannot be queried or explored. They need to be activated.\n\nTransferring TRX or TRC-10 tokens to an inactive account address will activate the account. Activating a new account on the Tron chain requires a fee of 1 TRX Cannot transfer TRX to yourself Cannot transfer 0 %s Account Not Active New TRON wallets require a deposit of at least 1 TRX to become active. Inactive wallets can hold and receive tokens but won’t correct balances until activated.
Ton Connect New Connection Invalid URL Address Unnamed URL Wallet Choose a Wallet Loading manifest… Your current wallet type %1$s does not support %2$s
WalletConnect URL Status By clicking Connect, you allow this app to view your public address. This is an important security step to protect your data from potential phishing risks. Failed to establish connection. Try reconnecting again. Invalid URL address Offline Online Connecting… New Connection Send Transaction Send Sign Request dApp Message to sign Sign You need to create or import a wallet before you can use WalletConnect Create a backup copy of the recovery phrase and the associated password that will allow you to recover your wallet if your phone is lost, stolen, broken, etc. Unsupported blockchain No suitable account No suitable EVM kit Unsupported chains: %s Unsupported methods: %s Unsupported events: %s Request not found You don\'t have any connected dApps Pending Requests Active Wallet Unnamed Pairings Paired dApps Delete All Do you want to delete all paired dApps? Delete Delete All Pairs # What are the risks when using WalletConnect?\n\nIt should be noted that WalletConnect merely acts as a bridge between the DeFi service website and the user\'s wallet (for example P.CASH).\n\nWhen a user initiates any action on the DeFi service website requiring a transfer of funds, P.CASH wallet presents a user with the confirmation window to approve/deny the respective transaction. The P.CASH wallet has little means to know the context of the transaction and merely acts as an instrument to allow/deny the transaction.\n\nIt\'s important to use a DeFi website provided by reliable entities. If a bad actor overtakes the website of the service, it\'s potentially possible for a user to be presented with a confirmation window going beyond the intents of the user without realising it. Therefore, only use well-regarded and widely used DeFi services.
Scan Adding tokens You need to scan card to add tokens Create wallet Create Error during creating wallets Couldn\'t load the card\'s certificate. Please try again later. Error during adding backup card
Unknown error, please try later Generate keys privately Your keys will be securely generated inside the chip. There is no seed phrase, which means nobody can export or steal it. Add backup Continue Add more backup cards Finalize backup No backup devices Add more backup devices To start the backup process add up to two backup cards. You can add more backup cards to increase security and flexibility. Create Access Code The access code protects your wallet card and ensures only you can use it. It’s required for important actions like making transactions or managing your wallet. Keep it safe and don’t share it with anyone. Choose any word, phrase, or number you want as your access code Access code Repeat access code Access code must be at least 4 symbols Access code confirmation does not match Prepare the backup card with number %s Primary card Backup card Change Access Code Reset to Factory Settings Forgot Access Code Reset Reset backup card Factory Reset will completely delete the wallet from the selected card. You will not be able to restore the current wallet or use the card or ring to recover the access code. I understand that after performing this action, I will no longer have access to the current wallet I realize that I can\'t use this card to recover my access code on the other cards of the current wallet Attention Another wallet has already been created on the card you\'re trying to add. If you have funds in this wallet, please withdraw it and then reset this card and add it as a backup. Tap the card #%s of the wallet Skip and Finish To change the access code tap the card or ring as shown above and do not remove until the end of the operation Access code recovery Disable this option if you don\'t want this card to be used to reset access codes on other cards or rings in this wallet. Please note that this will also prevent you from resetting the access code on this card. Allows you to use this card to reset access code on other cards in this wallet Tap the card Enabled Disabled Save
Warning Error Insufficient Balance The current %1$s balance is %2$s %1$s and below the amount required to process this transaction, including the transaction fee. Insufficient %s balance to complete this transaction including the transaction fee. Low Fee The set transaction fee amount is insufficient for processing this transaction now. Insufficient Liquidity Likely there is not enough liquidity available to process this trade. Try lowering the amount. Estimation Error Check balance to ensure there is enough %s to cover fee. Or try increasing price slippage amount and try again. Will auto-retry in 3 sec… Consider leaving some %s on balance to be able to pay for future transactions. Unexpected error: %s
Swap You Pay You Get (%s%%) %s%% Select Allowance Price Available Balance The current %s balance is lower than the amount required to process this transaction, including the transaction fee. Balance is lower than necessary. You might pay up to %s in fees. Balance for Fees Price Impact P.CASH has disabled swap action for this trade because you\'re getting an extremely unfavorable price. This is due to extremely low liquidity.\nIf you still want to swap please use %s website instead. Important! You\'re getting an extremely unfavorable price. This is due to extremely low liquidity. Guaranteed Amount Slippage Recipient No Providers No Quote Insufficient balance Out of min amount %s High price impact Wallet is Syncing Wallet Not Synced Next Select Token In Select Token Out Enter Amount Unlock Unlocking... Revoke Revoking... Create wallets Creating wallets.. You don\'t have all chosen wallets, need to create first You don\'t have all chosen wallets, need to create first,\nto exchange ZEC you need to create transparent address first Approve Approving Swapping Quoting Providers Via You Send You Receive Continue Later Cancel Swap The first swap has already been completed. The intermediate coins are in your wallet and will remain yours. Only the second swap step will be canceled. Swap %d Swap Provider (min) (max) You Pay You Get Confirm Quote expires in %d Quote expired Quote is invalid Fetching final quote Slippage is not applicable Minimum slippage applicable for this quote is %s%% No Slippage Control Cross-chain operations depend on third-party liquidity and delays, which may affect the final amount. Unsupported refund address Unsupported address Unexpected error Unsupported token Outbound Fee Liquidity Fee A temporary t-address is used when creating the exchange: %s. It is provided to the exchange provider and required for a refund if the exchange is not completed. Using a t-address may lead to partial deanonymization of the transaction.
Swap Settings Slippage Tolerance Your transaction will revert if the price changes unfavorably by more than this percentage Transaction Deadline Your transaction will revert if it is pending for more than this long. Recipient Address After the exchange operation, the amount will be transferred to the specified address Apply %s min
Zero Deadline Zero Slippage Your transaction is likely to fail. Slippage Tolerance can’t be more than %s%% Invalid address Invalid %s address Your transaction may be frontrun
Activate Token To receive %s, you need to activate the token. The Stellar network requires a trustline to be established for each token. Without it, your wallet cannot receive %s — the transaction will be rejected and the funds will not be delivered.
You Activate Activating Already Active The token is already active. Insufficient Balance You don\’t have enough XLM balance to activate this token.
You can exchange %s, or you need to revoke and approve the new amount Spender You Approve You Revoke
Unlock Access Allow access to the following amount Grant permission to a smart contract for token trading on your behalf, specifying the allowed amount. No impact on your balance, but incurs a small fee for approval.\n\nPre-approving a higher amount for future trades is cost-effective compared to on-demand approvals. Unlimited
Allowance The amount an exchange can spend on a user\’s behalf when executing token swaps. A proceeding transaction setting sufficient allowance is required before an actual swap transaction can take place. Price Impact The expected price deviation from a shown price, typically increases with the swap amount.
Receive %s
Transactions Transactions Hide You don\’t have any pending or past transactions yet Please wait for the sync to finish. Pending Received Change Trust Completed Processing Sent Burned Minted Approved Swapped Swap failed New Waiting Confirming Exchanging Sending Refunded Verifying Unknown Failed Contract Call External Call Contract Creation Contract Deploy to %s from %s unlimited %s unlimited All Blockchain Coin Contacts All Blockchains All Coins All Contacts Choose Coin Choose Blockchain Received Sent Swaps Approvals Unknown Transaction Operation Type Change Trust Create Account Ton Transaction Stellar Transaction Transaction can not be parsed Multiple Filter Hide suspicious tx Hiding incoming transactions with the dust amount in stablecoins, this partially solves the address poisoning attack problems. Filtering by contacts works on the following blockchains: Ethereum, BNB Smart Chain, Polygon, Avalanche, Gnosis, Fantom, Arbitrum, Optimism, Tron, Ton and ZCash. Below are the suitable contacts You don\'t have any suitable contacts
Transaction Info Status Date Sync Source ID Service Raw Transaction Contact Name To From Address Change\nAddress Spender Memo Locked until %s Unlocked at %s Recipient Historical Rate Fee Fee (est.) The transaction is sent to own address Double Spend Risk! View on %s Price Announced Amount Executed Amount Price Impact Executed Rate You Got You Sent Received Sent Approved Resend Speed Up This transaction is suspicious and can be a scam.\nPlease do not copy any addresses from this transaction. This action will attempt to invalidate the previous transaction by resending it with a higher fee.
Address Poisoning View Select the display style Standard Compact created by this wallet transaction suspicious transaction address is in the address book received from the blockchain
Got It Transaction Statuses Each transaction in your history is assigned a status based on a risk analysis system.\nThis helps identify potentially fraudulent operations and reduce the risk of sending funds to fake addresses. Always verify the full recipient address before sending funds, even if it appears in your history. The transaction may be part of an address poisoning attack. Always verify the full address before sending funds. The transaction was received from the network and may not have been created by this wallet. The sender or recipient is saved in your address book. The transaction was created by you and is genuine.
Address Poisoning Attack Be careful.\nThis transaction may be part of a fraudulent address spoofing attempt.
Be Careful This address may imitate an address you have previously used. If you copy it by mistake, funds may be sent to a different recipient. Verify the address before copying. Copy Anyway Don\'t Copy If you add it by mistake, you may later send funds to a different recipient. Verify the address before adding to contacts. Add Anyway Don\'t Add
What is Address Poisoning? Address Poisoning (Address Poisoning) - is a type of fraud where an attacker sends you a transaction from an address that looks similar to one you have previously used. The goal of the attack is to get into your transaction history so that in the future you might accidentally copy the fake address instead of the real one and send funds to the attacker. Such addresses may: match by the first and last characters; look almost identical in the list of recent transactions; masquerade as addresses you have previously used.
This address may be associated with an address poisoning attack. It could imitate an address you have previously used. Verify the full address before sending funds. I understand and accept the risk
Pending Processing Completed Failed The transaction has not been confirmed on the blockchain yet. Transactions sent with a recommended or higher fee setting are generally processed within a few minutes. Transactions sent with a low fee may remain pending for a few hours or even days, and can even be rejected. Note that status of an individual transaction in P.CASH wallet interface typically updated with a short delay. The transaction has been already included in the blockchain but has not reached permanent finality. At this point, it\'s safe to consider the transaction as completed for smaller payments. For larger payments, it\'s recommended to wait until the transaction status changes to completed. Transaction is completed and considered permanent and irreversible. The transaction did not get processed and no value transfer took place. Depending on the fail reason, some failed transactions may consume transaction fees. Transactions that were replaced or cancelled by another transaction do not consume transaction fees and will also appear sa failed. The P.CASH app is unable to show the reason for failed transactions, but users are able to look it up themselves on a public block explorer i.e. etherscan.io.
Speed Up Resend Cancel Cancel Transaction
Transaction already in block
Replaced Transactions Fee (Sat) Fee Too Low RBF not enabled Unable to replace
TimeLock The sender sent these funds with a spending lock that will expire on %s.\n\nNo worries, the received Bitcoins are already yours, but until the lock period expires you can not spend them on the Bitcoin network.
The Stellar network locks 1 XLM to activate the wallet and 0.5 XLM as a reserve for each token activated in your wallet. Currently locked: Wallet activation
Broadcast The transaction holding this amount is broadcasted but not yet accepted by the network.
Processing amount Transactions with this amount still syncing. And when they are confirmed, these tokens will be available for spending
Double Spend Double Spend Risk! There is another transaction on the blockchain that is trying to spend inputs used in this transaction. Only one transaction will be accepted by the network This Tx Conflicting Tx
Academy
Page not found
Base Currency OTHER Disclaimer The exchange rate data is provided by a third party service - Coingecko.com.\n\nThe P.CASH wallet app doesn\'t guarantee these values are always correct and matches market data. The chance for inconsistency is higher if you select any base currency other than %s.
Display Options Price Period % Change Price Change 1 Hour 1 Day 1 Week 1 Month 1 Year All Sum Rounding
Settings Security Base Currency Language Display Transactions Theme Terms Privacy Tell Friends P.CASH %s decentralized app PirateCash and Cosanta Foundation FAQ App Status App Cache Clear All Caches Clear Cache cleared Clearing caches may temporarily slow down the app while data is re-downloaded. Network Cache Cached API responses Image Cache Cached coin icons and images Database Cache Cached coin associations Market Prices Cache Cached coin prices and market data WalletConnect Ton Connect Rate Us Update App Launch Screen Appearance via Telegram via E-mail Auto-Lock Telegram https://t.me/PirateCash_ENG Twitter Follow us Join us on social media to be the first to discover project news, updates, and upcoming features!
Personal Support Enter your Telegram account name to start a personal support chat with our team. We\'ll get back to you and start working on your problem as soon as we see it. You\'ve already requested a private chat, find it on Telegram Account \@username Requested Request New Request Request failed Open Telegram
Together, with your support, we can make this app even better! Donate Mini App Connect Mini App +10% Bonus with Premium Connect mini-app with P.Cash Premium and receive +10% bonus to your game balance. MEV Protection
Recipient Check Checks the recipient address for risks before sending. Smart contract recipient check Checks the recipient address is not contract before sending. Check Address Select Blockchain Select Coin on %s Sanctions Check %s Blacklist Check Smart contract check Checks if the destination address is not a smart contract. AML Check Alpha AML is a tool for checking cryptocurrency wallets and transactions. It assesses the risk level of interacting with a wallet, identifies addresses on blacklists, sanctions lists, scam reports, and helps avoid receiving tainted crypto. alpha-aml.com Lowest Risk Low Risk High Risk Highest Risk You can always return to this feature in the premium settings. This is a premium feature for checking incoming transactions. It can be disabled in the premium settings section. Later Address Checker Choose address Unknown Low Risk Medium Risk High Risk Chainalysis.com Chainalysis is a blockchain analysis platform that provides tools for tracking, investigating, and assessing the risk of cryptocurrency addresses. It helps identify addresses associated with sanctions, fraud, and other illicit activities. Hashdit.io HashDit is a security platform that allows users to check wallet addresses for risks such as sanctions and blacklist status. It helps users avoid interacting with potentially malicious or blocked addresses. Allows you to verify whether a wallet address is blacklisted by Tether (USDT). If an address is blacklisted, any USDT tokens associated with it may be frozen, making them inaccessible and non-transferable. This check helps users avoid interacting with high-risk or restricted addresses. Enables you to check if a wallet address is blacklisted by the USDC issuer (Circle). Addresses on the blacklist may have their USDC holdings frozen due to compliance, fraud, or regulatory reasons. Using this check reduces the risk of sending funds to blocked or suspicious wallets. Provides a way to verify if a wallet address is blacklisted by PYUSD, issued by PayPal. Funds held at blacklisted addresses may be frozen and unusable, typically due to legal or security concerns. This tool helps ensure safer and compliant transactions with PYUSD. The address you entered appears to be invalid. Please double-check for typos or formatting issues and try again.
MARKETS TAB BALANCE TAB App Icon OUR ICON OTHER Balance Value Changing icon will close application Hide Markets Hide Buttons Balance Auto Hide Show All Transactions Hide Balance Transaction Auto Hide Passcode for transfer Display Nothing Last 4 transactions Last 2 transactions Last transaction Automatically hides balance each time the app is opened, regardless of previous preferences. Security Mode: hides transactions and allows you to set a separate passcode for unlocking them. This setting requires entering a passcode before sending assets, providing additional protection for your assets in case your device with an unlocked app falls into the wrong hands. Price Change %

P.CASH doesn\'t collect personal data that expose your private information i.e. coin balances or addresses. While we gather some UI usage statistics, it\'s solely for understanding our user base and app usage trends. That can be disabled if you wish. The wallet does not gather any personal data. There are no user accounts or databases storing user data. If allowed the wallet will share app usage habits with P.CASH team. This is to understand which features are being used (or not) by our users. Being a privacy focused app we need some way to evaluate our efforts and without this we have no idea whether the features we built are being used or not. Share crash data
Add New Added
RPC Url Add RPC Source Basic Auth(optional) RPC Source with this url already exists Entered url is invalid. Valid url must have one of the following schemes: https, wss
Custom peers (e.g., 188.40.241.115, dnsseed.custom.io) Blockchain Status After changing source the wallet will have to resync itself. Wallet is still syncing. Please wait for sync to complete before sending. Wallet sync failed. Tap Retry to reconnect. Choose the source P.CASH app should use for synchronizing wallet(s). Inputs/Outputs Transaction Settings Transaction Inputs / Outputs Make your BTC, LTC, BCH transactions harder to trace by changing the way transactions are structured. Most Bitcoin transactions, as well as transactions in alike cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin Cash, Dash, and Litecoin, generate two outputs. One output is the amount that goes to the receiver and the other is the change output that is returned to the sender. The way most wallets construct transactions makes it easy for a third party to understand which of the outputs went to the receiving party and which one was the change amount returned to the sender. As the output returned to the sender is later used in future transactions, a connection between these two transactions becomes apparent. \n\nThe P.CASH wallet implements measures to make it harder for someone to figure out which output goes where. \n\nThere are two options available to P.CASH users: \n\n1. Shuffle: The order of transaction outputs is randomized on every transaction. Sometimes change can be the first output, sometimes it can be the second. If a user trusts the developer of the app, then consider this a recommended option. \n\n2. Deterministic: There is a commonly agreed standard for ordering transaction outputs (known as BIP69). In open-source wallets, that standard ensures wallet users do not need to trust how developers of the app implement the ordering of the outputs. As this standard is new, not many wallets have implemented it yet. As a result, it\'s somewhat possible to see on the blockchain whether a transaction was sent from a wallet that uses that standard or not.
Info
Biometrics Set a Separate Passcode Enable Passcode Disable Passcode Delete a Separate Passcode Edit Passcode Set Duress Mode Edit Duress Passcode Disable Duress Passcode A specialized mode designed to keep selected wallets safe under coercion. Manage Wallets This action will restart P.CASH Wallet. Fully anonymize your internet while using this app. Note, in some cases WalletConnect feature for DeFi apps may be very slow or not work at all. Shuffle Random Indexing Deterministic Deterministic BIP69 Lexicographical Indexing Blockchair API Cosanta Explorer PirateCash Explorer Very Fast Hybrid Fast Blockchain Slow. Highest Privacy. Save Access Code Biometric authentication will be requested instead of the access code for interactions with your hardware wallet. Signed %s hashes
To use this feature you must set up an Android system PIN Enable System PIN Confirm Require system PIN Require setting up a system PIN to use the application. This significantly improves application security. Can only be configured during initial setup. Disabling will reset all wallets.
This action can\'t be undone.
To disable this feature you need to reinitialize the app.
System PIN Terms
Wallet access key will be permanently available to the app, even when device is locked.\nThis means an attacker can access data without additional verification.
Protection will only be at Android Keystore level, without additional authentication (PIN, biometrics).\nAlthough Keystore uses hardware protection, it doesn\'t replace user authentication.
In case of device compromise or app breach, private key and/or seed phrase may be stolen.\nThe app won\'t be able to prevent third-party access if they gain physical or software control over the device.
Responsibility for fund security lies entirely with the user.\nDevelopers are not responsible for access loss or fund theft due to disabled authentication.
Disabling authentication is not recommended for storing significant amounts.\nThis feature is only for users who consciously and voluntarily accept increased risks.
Duress Passcode Set a passcode for Duress Mode Edit Duress Passcode Enter new passcode for Duress Mode
Duress Mode This mode allows user to setup multiple unlock app passcodes where a desired passcode shows only specified wallets. Designed to keep selected wallets safe under coercion or threats. NOTES Biometrics The Biometric Authentication feature will work to unlock the Duress Mode. You can disable Biometric Authentication for convenience. Passcode Disabling Disabling the passcode in the main mode will automatically reset the duress mode. Passcode Change Changing the passcode in the Duress Mode will also change the current PIN code for that mode.
Select Wallets Select the wallets that will be displayed in duress mode. WALLETS WATCH WALLETS
Securely backup recovery phrases for each wallet. It\'s the only way to regain access to funds if the app malfunctions. The wallet recovery phrases are randomly generated on the device during setup and are not stored elsewhere. Disabling unlock PIN (code) on the smartphone deletes all wallets from the app. Recovery phrases will be needed to restore access to funds. Jailbreaking (rooting), using outdated OS, and installing apps from unknown sources may endanger the safety of funds. There may be undiscovered software issues in the code powering this app which may cause the app to malfunction.

Coin Manager Name, code or contract address No active account found Not supported by hardware card yet To create the wallet tap the card as shown above and do not remove until the end of the operation Add Token No results found. Try to add token manually if you know its smart contract address. There are 4 common address formats %1$s wallets can use to receive incoming payments: \n \n- BIP-44 (older) \n- BIP-49 \n- BIP-84 (recommended) \n- BIP-86 (newest) \n \nWhile P.CASH wallet supports all 4 of them it recommends to use a %2$s wallet operating in BIP-84 format. The wallets operating in this format get to pay lowest fees when sending %3$s transactions. There are 2 address formats Bitcoin Cash wallets can use to receive incoming payments: \n \n- TYPE0 (older) \n- TYPE145 (newer) \n \nWhile P.CASH wallet supports both of them it recommends to use a Bitcoin Cash wallet operating in TYPE145 format. Passphrase As a security measure we recommend not to use copy action on private key. I will risk it Don\'t copy
Backup Recovery Phrase Create a backup copy of the recovery phrase and the associated password that will allow you to recover your wallet if your phone is lost, stolen, broken, etc. Manual Backup Local Backup Later Complete one of the wallet backup options to start using the wallet. It\'s recommended to have a manual backup for each wallet.
Local Backup Save and Backup Set Password Backup Saved This password is used to encrypt backup file of your wallet. It can\'t be recovered or reset if lost or forgotten. To ensure data security, we recommend using a strong password for your backup — combining letters, numbers, and special characters.\nThe app allows you to create a backup with a simple password for your convenience, but you agree that the backup may be compromised if a simple password is used. Storage devices i.e. hard drives, USB drives , storage on smartphone etc. are all vulnerable to loss due to physical damage, theft or other unforeseen circumstances. I understand that theft or damage of a backup device will result in loss of a backup to a respective wallet. Duress Backup Duress Backup Duress backup uses deniable encryption. The backup file cannot reveal how many wallets it contains or if there is hidden data. The same backup decrypts differently depending on the password: a duress password opens only duress wallets, the main password opens all wallets. To use this feature, configure duress mode first. Duress Password Confirm Duress Password Main password and duress password cannot be the same. Password cannot be empty. No file manager available. Please install a file manager app to save backups.
Passphrase Terms Before activating the passphrase (BIP-39 Passphrase), please confirm that you understand: I understand that to access the funds, I must enter the passphrase exactly as it was created, with the same characters, spaces, and case. I understand that if the passphrase is lost or entered incorrectly, I will not be able to recover my wallet without its exact value. I understand that when restoring a wallet with an incorrect passphrase, the application will not display an error, but will create a new (empty) wallet. I understand that such a wallet can only be restored in applications that support the BIP-39 standard with a passphrase (e.g., Trezor), and most applications do not support this feature. I understand that developers do not have access to my data and cannot help me recover my wallet, as the application is non-custodial. I understand the risks and want to use the passphrase
I understand that <b>to access the funds, I must enter the passphrase</b> exactly as it was created, with the same characters, spaces, and case.
I understand that <b>if the passphrase is lost or entered incorrectly</b>, I will <b>not be able to recover my wallet</b> without its exact value.
I understand that <b>when restoring a wallet with an incorrect passphrase</b>, the application will not display an error, but will <b>create a new (empty) wallet</b>.
I understand that <b>such a wallet can only be restored in applications that support the BIP-39 standard with a passphrase</b> (e.g., Trezor), and most applications <b>do not support this feature</b>.
I understand that <b>developers do not have access to my data</b> and cannot help me recover my wallet, as the application is <b>non-custodial</b>.
<b>I understand the risks and want to use the passphrase</b>

Blockchain Purpose Account Tap to show private key To do
Account xPrivKey Account xPubKey
Private Keys EVM Private Key Grants full control over EVM based crypto i.e Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain etc. within respective wallet. Stellar Secret Key Grants full control over Stellar account within respective wallet. BIP32 Root Key Grants full control over the assets on the respective wallet. Account Extended Private Key Grants full control over Bitcoin and other UTXO based crypto i.e. Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, Dash etc. within respective wallet. Never share this key with anyone. P.CASH Wallet support team will never ask for it.
<p><b>We have converted your seed phrase from %d words to the Monero standard — 25 words.</b></p> <p>To restore your wallet in our app, use the original <b>%d-word</b> phrase.</p> </p>If you want to restore the same wallet in another app or service that does not support a non-standard number of words, use the <b>25-word Monero format phrase</b> provided below.
Public Keys EVM Address Account Extended Public Key Allows read-only monitoring of wallets holding assets on Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain and other EVM based blockchains. Allows read-only monitoring of wallets holding Bitcoin and other UTXO based crypto (i.e Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, Dash etc.). ZCash Unified Full Viewing Key Allows read-only monitoring of ZCash, transparent and shielded transactions for the wallet.

Trading Volume Powered by Defiyield.app Issues: %d Treasuries Filters Powered by Bitcointreasuries.net Reports Funds Invested Overview Contracts Blockchains BIPs Coin Types Links Guide Website Whitepaper Reddit Twitter No twitter available No tweets yet Quoted %s Retweeted %s Replying to %s See on Twitter Telegram Github Market Cap Diluted MCap In Circulation Total Supply K M B T Q Vol No Data No data available Add To Wallet In Wallet Favorite Unfavorite 1 Week 1 Month Show More Show Less Inception Date Total Value Locked in Top 10 Holders Total Holders: %s Lead [%s]\nNot found Indicators Moving Averages Oscillators The EMA, SMA, and WMA are moving averages used in technical analysis:\n\nEMA emphasizes recent prices for quicker reactions.\nSMA averages price data for a general trend view.\nWMA balances sensitivity and noise reduction by linearly weighting recent data. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is a momentum oscillator that measures price speed and change, identifying overbought (above 70) or oversold (below 30) market conditions. It can also detect price reversals through divergences. The Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) is a momentum indicator that tracks the relationship between two EMAs of a security\'s price. It signals buying or selling opportunities when the MACD line (12-period EMA minus 26-period EMA) crosses the 9-period EMA, known as the signal line. Type Period RSI Length Fast Length Slow Length Signal Smoothing Indicators available only for P.CASH Wallet premium users. This is an AI-generated description based on the provided reference material for the given cryptocurrency. It may contain errors. VERIFIED Verified All
// Coin Page -> Analytics Total trading volume for the token on leading centralized exchanges over a 30-day period. Chart showing variation in daily trading volume for the token on leading centralized exchanges over 1 year period. Token\'s rank is based on trading volume on leading centralized exchanges over a 30-day period. List of all tokens ranked based on trading volume on centralized exchanges over 24H / 7D / 1M intervals.
Total trading volume for the token on leading decentralized exchanges over 30-day period. Chart showing variation in daily trading volume for the token on leading decentralized exchanges over 1 year period. Token\'s rank is based on trading volume on leading decentralized exchanges over a 30-day period. List of all tokens ranked based on trading volume on decentralized exchanges over 24H / 7D / 1M intervals.
DEXes that are being tracked: Ethereum : Uniswap V2/3, Sushiswap Binance-Smart-Chain : PancakeSwap
Total currently available liquidity for the token on leading decentralized exchanges. Chart showing variation in available liquidity for the token on leading decentralized exchanges over 1 year period. List of all tokens ranked based on available liquidity for the token on leading decentralized exchanges.
DEXes that are being tracked: Ethereum : Uniswap V2/3, Balancer V1/2, Bancor V2, Curve, Sushiswap Binance-Smart-Chain : PancakeSwap, DODO V1/2
Total number of unique daily active addresses over 24-hour period. Chart showing variation in daily active address count over 1 year period. Total number of unique blockchain addresses transacting with token over 30-day period. Token\'s rank based on the number of active wallets transacting with the token 30-day period. List of all tokens ranked based on the number of daily active addresses transacting with the token over 24h / 7D / 1M intervals.
Total number of unique blockchain transactions with token over a 30-day period. Chart showing variation in transaction count over 1 year period. Token\'s rank based on the number of transactions with the token 30-day period. List of all tokens ranked based on the number of transactions with the token over 24h / 7D / 1M intervals. The total number of tokens transferred over the blockchain over the 30 day period.
Total number of unique addresses holding the token on various blockchains. Top 10 wallets holding the token on each blockchain. Tracked blockchains: Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain, Optimism, Arbitrum, Celo, Cronos, Avalanche, Fantom, Polygon
Total-Value-Locked (or Assets Under Management) in the project\'s smart contracts. Chart showing variation Total-Value-Locked in project\'s smart contracts over 1 year period. Token\'s rank is based on current Total-Value-Locked. List of all tokens ranked based on current Total-Value-Locked. Market Cap / TVL ratio for the project.
This project has no analytical data ••• CEX Volume CEX Volume Rank Tokens ranked by trading volume for the token on centralized exchanges. DEX Volume DEX Volume Rank Tokens are ranked by trading volume for the token on decentralized exchanges. DEX Liquidity DEX Liquidity Rank Tokens are ranked by available liquidity on decentralized exchanges. Daily Active Addresses Active Addresses Rank Tokens are ranked by a number of unique addresses transacting with the token. Transaction Count Tx Count Rank Holders Project TVL Project TVL (Total Value Locked) M.Cap / TVL Ratio Project Revenue Project Fee Project Revenue Rank Project Fee Rank Holders Rank Tokens are ranked by revenue generated for holders via mechanisms i.e. staking or token burns. Tokens are ranked according to fees generated by respective projects. The way fees are collected varies from project to project. Ranking tokens by unique addresses holding them on multiple blockchains. Other Data Reports Funding Treasuries Audits last 30d current Rank 30-Day Rank 30-Day Unique Addresses 30-Day Volume Blockchain 1 Blockchain 2 Blockchain 3 Overall Score Excellent Good Fair Poor The overall score is based on the average daily trading volume on centralized exchanges over the last 7 days. The overall score is based on the average daily trading volume on decentralized exchanges over the last 7 days. The overall score is based on the total available liquidity on decentralized exchanges. The overall score is based on the average daily active addresses over the last 7 days. The overall score is based on the total value locked (assets under management) on the project represented by the given token. The overall score is based on the average daily transaction count over the last 7 days. The overall score is based on the total number of addresses holding the respective tokens. 30 minutes 1 hour 4 hours 8 hours 1 day 1 week 1 month Strong Buy Strong Sell Sell Buy Neutral Smart Contract Analysis High Risk Items Medium Risk Items Risky Attention Required Powered by De.Fi %d Items
Trading Assistant Always remember to apply risk management, and note that this is not financial advice. overbought oversold down up The actions with the asset are risky. Starting from the %s The asset is outside the Bollinger Band channel and %s. RSI = %1$s, This also indicates that the asset is %2$s. There might be a strong %sward movement, so it\'s better to wait for the asset price to return to the channel. the asset was %s, but now it has returned to the Bollinger Band channel. This indicates a possible trend reversal. Meanwhile, the RSI is %1$s, which still indicates that it is %2$s. This could be a very strong signal to enter the market. Keep in mind that there may be several attempts of %sward movement after returning to the channel, so do not forget about risk management. Meanwhile, the RSI is %s, which also indicates a trend reversal (RSI crossed the boundary at 70\%%). the asset was in the overbought/oversold zone, but at the moment the price has returned to the Bollinger Band channel in the neutral zone. The RSI is %s also confirms the absence of a strong trend, so overall the asset price is moving towards averaging and further movement is possible in any direction. In general, the asset price is moving towards averaging and further movement is possible in any direction. Please note: above below growth decrease EMA 200. Determines the overall sentiment and trend. The daily price of the asset is located %1$s the EMA (%2$s). This means that globally the asset is set for %3$s. above below MACD. Assesses the strength of the trend considering the average price change. The daily value of the histogram is %1$s (%2$s). The price of the asset globally may move %3$s. Technical Indicators We use the Bollinger Bands + RSI strategy to determine trading signals. All calculations are based on daily candlesticks and provide advice for a moderately long term. The essence of the strategy is that the asset price should reach an extreme, breaking out of the Bollinger Bands channel, and the RSI should be in the overbought/oversold zone. After the price returns to the channel, there is a high probability of the price returning to the mean values or attempting to break the channel from the other side. Note that the strategy may give several false signals during strong market movements before a correct signal appears.\n\nPlease remember that it is very important to apply risk management to trading and remember to cut losses if the market situation changes! Show Details Hide Details Strong Buy Buy Neutral Sell Strong Sell Risky

Sync Error Retry Change Source Report Sync error Failed to fetch data from the current source. You can retry or switch to a different sync source. Failed to fetch data from the current source. Please try again later. Failed to fetch data from current source.\n\nYou can:\n• Try again later\n• Enable VPN and retry sync\n\nIf you received a 403 error, your IP address may be blocked. Using a VPN usually resolves this issue.
Show changelog after update JOIN P.CASH Activate
Choose Type No item activity yet To make sure that this widget works properly, you should enable Autostart.
Android Keystore Error Your wallet encryption keys were invalidated by Android security system. This can happen when fingerprint or lock screen settings change.\n\nTo restore access, re-import your wallet using your backup phrase.\n\nIf this happens frequently, try disabling Google Smart Lock in device settings and restart your phone. Warning It looks like your device uses a non-default keyboard. P.CASH recommends selecting default keyboard on your device as third party keyboards can potentially collect and expose users\' input data. \n\nClick \'Skip\' button if you would like to keep using your preferred keyboard. The P.CASH team recommends running app only on non-rooted (not jailbroken) phones.\n\nPlease note when you use a rooted device the app can no longer guarantee the safety of your cryptocurrency from malicious software.
Phone PIN This app requires that phone has the passcode (screen lock) enabled.\n\n You may enable it in Android settings.\n\nPlease note that when you disabled the PIN on the OS level the security measures in safe storage of your phonе made the previously stored data invalid. You will need to Restore your wallet keys to get back to your wallet. This app requires your phone\'s PIN in order to keep sensitive data from your wallet in Android\'s secure storage and not in the app itself.

Premium About Premium Advanced Security Create Hidden Wallet Hidden Wallet Passcode Create PIN for Hidden Wallet Terms The global wallet will not see this wallet, and if you forget your PIN, you can restore access to the wallet using the seed phrase.
The user understands that the wallet will not be visible among other wallets.
The user understands that if they forget their PIN, the wallet can be restored by knowing its seed phrase.
Secure Reset Secure Reset PIN Create PIN for Secure Reset Terms Read the terms and conditions carefully. To use this feature, please backup all wallets.
Entering the specified PIN during authorization will result in the deletion of all information, including wallets.
If you have not made backup copies of the private keys for all wallets, you will lose access to all your funds.
The user is aware of the risks and understands that this action will be irreversible.
After reset, your main PIN becomes your reset PIN, and the reset PIN will be turned off.
Premium settings Try for FREE Upgrade your experience with Premium features! ⭐️ Premium is available Demo Premium: %d days left Select Wallet for Demo Choose a wallet to activate demo premium features. Only one wallet can have demo premium at a time. Demo Premium activated successfully! Login to the app Subscription has expired To use the features in this section, you need to activate your subscription.\n\nYou can hide this window by disabling all features in this section. Delete data Are you sure you want to delete all logs? Are you sure you want to delete this record? If logging is enabled, you can view the login history in Advanced Security.
Send SMS via ZEC This mode allows you to notify your close friends or lawyer that you have been forced to open the app. This mode works if you have ZEC in your wallet. If you don\'t, the app will add it. Make sure you have the required amount in the selected wallet. Syncing to send… Your message has been successfully sent. An error occurred while sending your message. Insufficient balance Transparent addresses are not allowed. Please use a shielded (z) address. You cannot send notifications to your own wallet address. Please enter a different recipient address. Choose Wallet None Required coins for transaction Please specify the address where the message should be sent. Test SMS No wallets with ZEC tokens found
%1$d option
%1$d options
%1$d entry
%1$d entries

Cannot create log file. Please ensure you have enough storage space.
Safety Rules Before exporting your wallet, please read the rules carefully:<br/><br/><b>1. Your seed phrase and private keys are yours alone.</b><br/>Support will <b>never</b> ask for this information. If you share it with third parties, <b>you will permanently lose access to your funds</b>.<br/><br/><b>2. Do not store your seed digitally.</b><br/>Screenshots, photos, notes, and other electronic storage methods are not secure. Write down your seed phrase on paper and store it in a safe place.<br/><br/><b>3. The P.cash team does not have access to your keys.</b><br/>We do not store and cannot recover your seed phrase or private keys if they are lost. You are solely responsible for keeping them safe. Points to agree with: I understand that the seed phrase is confidential and should not be disclosed to third parties. I understand that I cannot store my seed phrase in digital form. I understand that the p.cash team does not have access to my keys and cannot recover them.
Connect Mini App Step 1 To connect, you need to create or import a wallet. Please choose your wallet with premium subscription +10% Bonus with Premium Checking availability %s You don\'t have %s Add
Step 2 Please accept the user terms Collection of technical data on the device The app will collect a limited set of technical data about the device once when linking the wallet to the game, including: data from certain sensors (e.g., gyroscope and accelerometer), battery status and whether charging is connected, model and basic characteristics of the device. This data is used exclusively to generate a unique technical fingerprint for the device as part of SWAP 5 (protection against multiple accounts and abuse). Important: data collection occurs exactly twice and only at the user\'s initiative, the app does not collect data in the background, the data is not used to track the user, all collected data is automatically deleted from the server after SWAP 5 is completed, data is not transferred to third parties. I agree to the terms and conditions and give permission to create a digital fingerprint of the device. Step 4 Enter the code from the image Update Send Wrong code, please try again Captcha expired, please refresh QR code expired, please scan again Link expired, please use new one from mini app to start connection Open Mini App Step 5 Special proposal for premium members Activate Premium in P.CASH and guaranteed receive from due to the increased budget distribution coefficient.
